name: Check N Title Loans
description_short: Texas-based lender offering vehicle title loans up to $50,000 and cash advance loans up to $1,500, available online or in-store with same-day funding.
description_long:
Check N Title Loans (CNT) is a Texas-based consumer lender operating storefront locations in the Dallas-Fort Worth area, including a location on Abram Street in Arlington. The company operates under a dual-channel model, allowing borrowers to either visit a physical branch or complete an application entirely online. Their brand positions around speed and accessibility for consumers who need cash quickly and may not qualify for traditional bank products.

CNT's primary product is vehicle title loans, which allow borrowers to use their car's title as collateral for loans up to $50,000 while continuing to drive the vehicle. Their secondary product is short-term cash advance loans of up to $1,500, which can be initiated and funded remotely. Applications are organized into four distinct tracks: online title loan, storefront title loan, online cash advance, and storefront cash advance, giving borrowers flexibility in how they engage.

Several features distinguish CNT from typical title and payday lenders. Customer reviews across dozens of entries consistently cite processing times under 20 minutes for in-store visits. The company operates a referral program that existing customers recommend. Multiple reviewers specifically mention a zero-interest rate offer available to first-time borrowers or qualifying members—an uncommon promotional structure in this lending segment. Staff are mentioned by name with frequency, suggesting consistent service quality at individual branches.

Check N Title Loans meets a real demand for fast, collateral-backed emergency cash, particularly for car owners who need more than a standard payday loan can provide. However, the website discloses no APR, fee structure, or repayment terms for either product—a material transparency gap that requires prospective borrowers to request full cost disclosures in person or online before committing. Title loans carry meaningful risk: default can result in vehicle repossession, which can worsen the underlying financial situation. This lender is best approached when other options have been exhausted and the borrower has a clear repayment plan.
address: 1606 E Abram St Suite 104, Arlington, TX 76010

company_info.headquarters: 1606 E Abram St Suite 104, Arlington, TX 76010

pros: Title loans available up to $50,000 — higher ceiling than most payday-adjacent lenders
Cash advance loans up to $1,500 with home-based online application option
Both online and in-store application tracks available for each product type
Zero-interest offer for first-time or qualifying member borrowers (per customer reviews)
Referral program available to existing customers
In-store processing frequently completed in under 20 minutes per customer reviews
Borrowers keep and drive their vehicle while title loan is outstanding
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee schedules disclosed anywhere on the website
Title loan default risk: vehicle can be repossessed if loan is not repaid
Cash advance loans capped at $1,500 — insufficient for larger emergency needs
Geographic footprint appears limited to Texas (Dallas-Fort Worth area)
No information on loan terms, repayment schedules, or rollover policies
best_for: Car owners who need emergency cash above $1,000 and want to keep driving their vehicle
Consumers who need small short-term cash advances under $1,500 with same-day funding
Borrowers who prefer in-person service and local storefront accessibility
Repeat borrowers who can qualify for the zero-interest member promotional rate
diagnosis: Check N Title Loans is best for Texas residents who own a vehicle and need emergency cash quickly — especially amounts between $1,000 and $50,000 that exceed what a payday lender offers. The main caveat is that no rates or fees are disclosed publicly, so borrowers must request a full cost breakdown before signing, and must have a clear repayment plan given the repossession risk on title loans.
services: Vehicle title as collateral (vehicle retained by borrower)
First-time or member zero-interest promotional offer
In-person loan counseling and Q&A at branch
